patient.instructions:

Start with: “I can’t shake this cough and I get out of breath way too easily.” If asked what’s going on, volunteer: dry cough ~6 weeks, winded on one flight of stairs, low-grade evening fevers, fatigue, poor appetite with a few pounds lost. Express misconceptions: “It’s probably stubborn pneumonia,” “maybe asthma/GERD/COVID,” and worry about a blood clot after a long car ride. Emphasize cost/job fears; push for the cheapest plan and minimal time off. If the clinician shows empathy, soften and share more.

required_evidence: Subacute cough and dyspnea over weeks

Nonresolving pneumonia despite appropriate antibiotics

non_pharmacological: Stop unnecessary antibiotics if bacterial infection unlikely

Start with: “I just can’t sleep, and it’s been going on for months.” Volunteer only: long time to fall asleep, waking many times, unrefreshed sleep, daytime exhaustion and poor focus at work.
